Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Analyze the Request Input A detailed natural language description of a multinational supply chain process for a consumer electronics manufacturer smartphones Task Generate 20 thought provoking open ended questions about this process Focus Discovering insights potential issues strategies for improvement and implications of decisions Constraint Do not provide any SQL queries Just list the questions

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Source Text Process Description Stages RD to Sourcing International suppliers Taiwan Japan South Korea Germany US to Assembly Malaysia 24 7 automated workers to Logistics Air Sea customs insurance to Distribution Regional DCs Europe NA Asia LatAm to Last Mile Retailers E commerce Direct Key Factors Lead times quality metrics contractual terms JIT vs aggregated shipments inventory tracking sales forecasts QA protocols compliance environmental labor regulatory warranty after sales recalls continuous improvement cost lead time carbon footprint Systems Data Multiple currencies time zones regulatory frameworks digital systems POs invoices customs scheduling demand planning analytics ML for forecasting Goal Timely cost effective delivery quality safety compliance

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Brainstorming Categories for Questions Risk Management Resilience Sustainability Ethics Technology Data Inventory Demand Planning Supplier Relationships Logistics Transportation Customer Experience After sales Financial Implications

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Drafting Questions Iterative Process

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Draft 1 Risk What happens if Taiwan stops shipping chips Refinement How does the company mitigate geopolitical risks associated with relying heavily on a single region for critical components like microprocessors

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Draft 2 Sustainability How do they reduce carbon footprint Refinement What trade offs exist between minimizing carbon footprint through sea freight and meeting consumer demand for rapid delivery via air freight

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Draft 3 ML Is the ML good Refinement In what ways might over reliance on machine learning forecasting models obscure emerging market trends that human intuition could catch

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Draft 4 JIT Is JIT risky Refinement How does the just in time replenishment strategy impact the supply chains resilience during unexpected global disruptions such as pandemics or port strikes

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Draft 5 Suppliers How do they manage quality Refinement What mechanisms are in place to ensure consistent quality standards across suppliers operating under different regulatory frameworks and cultural norms

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Draft 6 Assembly What about workers in Malaysia Refinement How does the company monitor and enforce labor compliance standards at the assembly plant in Malaysia amidst varying international labor laws

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Draft 7 Currency Money changes value Refinement How do fluctuations in multiple currencies affect the overall cost structure and what hedging strategies are employed to protect margins

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Draft 8 Recalls What if phones break Refinement What is the protocol for executing a global product recall efficiently while minimizing brand damage and logistical costs

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Draft 9 Data Is data shared Refinement How is data privacy and security managed when sharing sensitive supply chain information across multiple digital systems and international partners

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Draft 10 Direct vs Retail Why sell direct Refinement What are the logistical challenges associated with scaling direct to consumer shipments compared to traditional retailer distribution models

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Draft 11 Inventory Too much stock Refinement How does the company balance the cost of holding excess inventory in slower moving markets against the risk of stockouts in high demand regions

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Draft 12 RD Does RD talk to supply chain Refinement How early are supply chain constraints integrated into the RD design phase to prevent manufacturability issues later

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Draft 13 Customs Customs slow things down Refinement What strategies are used to navigate varying customs regulations and tariffs without significantly delaying time to market

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Draft 14 Automation Robots vs Humans Refinement How does the balance between automated machinery and human workers in Malaysia affect flexibility versus efficiency during demand spikes

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Draft 15 Returns What about e waste Refinement What processes exist for handling reverse logistics specifically regarding e waste recycling and refurbishment of returned units

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Draft 16 Supplier Dependency Too many eggs in one basket Refinement To what extent does dependency on specific suppliers for critical components like OLED screens create a bottleneck risk

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Draft 17 Performance Metrics How do we measure success Refinement Which key performance indicators KPIs are most critical for balancing cost efficiency with service level agreements across different regions

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Draft 18 Communication Time zones hard Refinement How does the organization facilitate real time communication and decision making across multiple time zones during a supply chain crisis

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Draft 19 Contractual Contracts rigid Refinement How flexible are contractual terms with international suppliers when sudden changes in demand require rapid scaling up or down

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Draft 20 Future Proofing What about new tech Refinement How does the supply chain adapt to technological obsolescence given the rapid lifecycle of consumer electronics

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Review and Refine Check count 20 questions Check type Open ended thought provoking Check constraints No SQL just list Check relevance All tied to the provided text

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Self Correction on Question 3 Make sure it sounds more strategic In what ways is good Self Correction on Question 15 Ensure it ties to the environmental mention in the text Self Correction on Question 1 Ensure it covers the multinational aspect

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Final Polish Ensure variety in starting words How What To what extent In what ways Ensure they cover different stages RD Sourcing Assembly Logistics Distribution After sales

Name: Deductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Final Output Generation Proceed to generate the list based on the refined drafts Geopolitical risk JIT resilience Carbon vs Speed ML vs Human Insight Quality consistency Labor compliance Currency hedging Recall protocol Data security DTC vs Retail Inventory balancing RD Integration Customs navigation Automation flexibility Reverse logistics Supplier dependency KPI balancing Cross timezone comms Contract flexibility Obsolescence management Looks solid I will write them out clearly

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Final Check against Constraints 20 questions Yes Thought provoking Open ended Yes No SQL Yes Based on text Yes Ready to write
